content:
Introduction Scrcpy, Abberivated from "Screen Copy" is an application that provides Mirroring and Controlling capabilities to an Android Device via USB or over A Network. It is a Multi-Platform, Open Source, and Free Application. Your Android needs to be at least Android 5.0, also you need to enable USB or ADB debugging on your Android. Some Users might need to enable additional settings to control Mouse and Keyboard.
Installation You can view Scrcpy's Github to read its Installation and Usage Guide. Installation Commands : Using choco, choco install adb choco install scrcpy Features : Recording It features Recording of Mirrored Android Screen, It will be a great tool to record Games, without degrading Device Performance. As Video is recorded on Computer. It can also add Buffering to the Device. This might increase Latency but reduces jitter. Read More about Recording via Scrcpy.
Connectivity As Scrcpy uses ADB to communicate with the Device, We can also Mirror Screens over the same Network with TCP/IP. If your Device is Android 11+ you can just use Wireless Debugging. If Wireless Connections aren't Stable you can decrease the bit rate and the resolution. Also, you can Connect your Device to Remote Devices by tunneling the Connections. Read More about Scrcpy's Connectivity.
Window Cnfiguration We can also configure, How Windows are displayed while Screens are Mirrored. You can configure the Title of the Window, Window Size, Borders, Device Rotation, Touches, Stay-Awake, Fullscreen, etc. It also supports Input Controls, Where it syncs the Android's Clipboard and PC's Clipboard. Which can be useful for Editing Documents. Gestures like Pich to Zoom are also Supported. You can simulate your Keyboard or Mouse as physically connected to your Android. Read More about Window Configuration.
Input Control Input Controls, Lets you choose How to Control your Android from Computer using scrcpy. OTG Mode also exists, Where you can simulate your PC's Mouse and Keyboard connected to the Android. But, while your device is in OTG mode, It is unable to support Screen Mirroring. It can help a lot in Gaming on Android, As supported games also support in-game Keyboard controls Similar to Gaming on a PC. Here is a little Video of me trying to play Minecraft, Where I ran scrcpy --otg on The Command Prompt. Which will prompt a Scrcpy-Iconed Window, by clicking on that Window a Cursor will appear on your Connected Android. The Keyboard will be automatically configured according to the Game. Read More about Input ControlsRead More about Input Controls.
File Drop Scrcpy, Also supports a File Drop feature, Where you can Install Applications on your Android just by Dropping apk Files from the Computer in the Screen Mirroring Window. There will be No Visual Feedback on the Android or the Mirrored Screen, But a Log will be Printed on the Terminal. You can also Send Files by Dragging files to the Mirrored Screen Window. Read More about File Drop.
Keyboard Shortcuts Scrcpy also supports these Keyboard Shortcuts, If you don't want to use Terminal. but, if you want to keep things simple and want to use a GUI to control scrcpy. Read More about Keyboard Shortcuts.
GUIscrcpy You can use guiscrcpy, It is a Simple, Pluggable, Multi-Platform, Graphical User Interface for scrcpy. It is written entirely in Python, You can check its GitHub Page for more Instructions. You can Install it with Scoop Package Manager. scoop bucket add extras scoop install guiscrcpy
Conclusion All in All, scrcpy is an Award Winning Screen Mirroring Tool. It's a great alternative to those Propritrary Screen Mirroring Software. I think there are really great Projects you can do with Screen Mirroring. It just takes your Old Smartphone and Machine to reuse. Find the Beauty of using your Hardware to the Limits with me.
title: Screen Mirroring

content:
Introduction Browsers, The Holy Portal to the Internet. Nowadays, People mostly use Laptops or PCs to access the Internet via Web Browsers. Most Machines are used to Browse or Edit Documents. Now, Browsers do Support a lot of Functionalities. So, they need to be Features Rich. As a result, they become Chunky. In this Blog, I won't be talking about CLI(Command Line Interface) based Browsers. As, They are really complex to use, even for me. So, I prefer using GUI(Graphical User Interface) based Browsers. Most browsers today are based on Chromium, and only a few Gekko and Webkit-based Browsers exist.
Source: StatCounter Global Stats - Browser Market Share
How Web Browsers work ? A Browser is used to locate, retrieve and display content on the World Wide Web, including Web pages, images, video, and other files. The browser runs on the Client's Computer, Contacts the Web server, and requests information. The Web server sends the response back to the Web browser, Which displays the results on the computer or other Internet-enabled device that supports a browser. Now, The Browser and Server communicate with each other basically in HTML to reduce the strain of Downloading larger files. It's the Web Browser's Job to compile the Code and present it to the Client in the form of a Webpage. So, A browser needs to be Feature Rich and must support most Elements and Languages.
Selection Basis What Browsers should you use then? In this list, I will be focusing on Free & Open Source, Privacy respecting browsers. Also, I will add a Minimalistic Browser for those Slow PCs. As most browsers are Chromium based you can easily Import Settings once you set up any one Chromium based browser. But I suggest reviewing all Settings as different browsers provide different Functionalities and Features. You can just Google what a Function does and Configure your browser according to your needs. Also, I will add Installation Commands of winget or choco at the end of every Browser's Description.
Google Chrome Now, I know you all absolutely love using Google Chrome. I Admit that Chrome is Optimized for most Hardware, and It has Improved itself over time. Also, Google Services works best in Chrome, and you get A variety of Services from Google. But it still Hogs up a lot of System Resources and is stuffed with telemetry. So, I won't even consider using that Proprietary piece of Shit.
Chromium Chromium is an Open Source Browser that is the Base for most browsers. Yup, Google Chrome, and most other browsers are based on Chromium. It's Open Source, and Unlike Google Chrome it doesn't include Telemetry Stuff. Still, It works just like Google Chrome and Integrates well with Google Services and Websites. Installation Command : winget install -e --id Hibbiki.Chromium
Ungoogled Chromium Ungoogled Chromium is just like Chromium, but without Google Services baked in. You can use Google Services and other Functionalities, But it just doesn't bake in those services by default. As a result, It doesn't Bloat itself and decreases Resource Usage. It features tweaks to enhance privacy, transparency, and control. all in all, if you don't want to share your data with Google you can use Ungoogled-Chromium. Installation Command : winget install -e --id eloston.ungoogled-chromium
Brave Browser Brave, This Browser is the best Browser for Normies. If you don't want to configure a browser to block Ads and respect your privacy, Brave is the best Option. If you configure it a bit, It works like a Charm. If you just disable the Crypto Stuff, it works really great. I would really suggest you Configure the Browser once. It Blocks Advertisements from every single Website. It prevents Trackers and Fingerprinting too. Installation Command : winget install -e --id BraveSoftware.BraveBrowser
Firefox Firefox is an Open Source, A Non-Profit Organisation Browser. It is based on a different Web Engine than Chromium, It is one of the Few browsers not based on Chromium. It tries to Respect User Privacy, Block Trackers, and works seamlessly with Google Services. As Google is the Largest Donator for Mozilla. Mozilla tries to favor its Decision to Google's Advantage, allows Google Trackers, and keeps the Default Search Engine as Google.
Google provides Mozilla with Funds to continue its Monopoly, By Funding Mozilla Google can, Avoid Law Suits and keep its Monopoly. The State of Mozilla in terms of Funding is pretty bad, Where Mozilla earned nearly 500 million in donations. Where 450 million were donated by Google. So, respecting Google is a necessity for Firefox. Installation Command : winget install -e --id Mozilla.Firefox
Waterfox So, If not Firefox then who? Waterfox is a Firefox Fork which is more Private and not biased toward any Corporation. Even though, It is a Fork of Firefox it has enhanced its Privacy and Trackers Blocking. Also, No Telemetry Data is Collected. It's a Faster Browser than Firefox as no data collection or telemetry is Enabled. Developers try to optimize it as per their needs. Installation Command : winget install -e --id Waterfox.Waterfox
Min Browser Now, We need a Minimalistic Browser for Slower PCs, Min is a browser based on Chromium and also written with CSS and JS. It has a really minimalistic Interface, Ad-blocking, and Script blocking capabilities. With Tab Grouping features, a Reading Feature, Focus Mode, and Keyboard Shortcut Support. In Short, it's a great browser for low-end Machines and for Productivity Users too. Installation Command : choco install min.portable
Conclusion In Conclusion, I would say, Browsers are an essential part of a PC these days, and having a Private, Secure, and Dependable Browser is really Important. and I hope this list will help you to choose one. Just remember to configure them according to your needs.
description: Browsers, The Holy Portal to the Internet.
title: Browsers

content:
Introduction Installing Applications in Windows isn't Secure. We either download executable files from some Websites or from The Microsoft Store where many Applications are Paid or Unavailable. Also, Installing Executables from some Random Websites, Makes updating them a pain in the ass and nor these Executable Files Verified. Also with The Microsoft Store, You don't get the entire fleet of Applications to Install. So, What is the Solution?
Package Managers Package Managers are Primarily Software to Manage your Applications, Just like The Proprietary Microsoft Store in Windows. On the other hand, Linux uses Package Managers like apt, yum, DNF, Pacman, etc. But, Following the FOSS Ideology, We will be using a Free and Open Source Package Manager for Windows. Which can Install, Uninstall or Update Applications for free. These Package Managers basically, Have a Huge Repository of Executable Files and, When we ask our Package Manager to Install an Application, They Download the Executable File and Install it. Similarly, They can Update and Uninstall the Packages too.
Winget Now, Microsoft has created an Open Source Tool that is a Replacement for The Microsoft Store which is also known as Winget (Windows Package Manager), Microsoft Store has a Huge Repository of Applications and this Utility helps a lot in managing packages in Windows. Kudos to Microsoft for that ! Its Installation Process is really easy, and Here is its Installation Guide and, its Usage Guide by Microsoft. You can also use winget.run to Find Packages and their Install Commands. Here is a little GIF of the Installation Process of yt-dlp with winget.
Chocolatey Now, Some Applications aren't supported by Winget. So, We can use Chocolatey which is similar to Winget but supports more open-source packages. You can visit this website's Install Page and Usage Page for choco(CLI). Also, there is a GUI for Choco too, which will be similar to the Microsoft Store. Here is its Installation Guide. They also have a Find Packages Page, which can be used to find Applications and their details. Here is Another GIF of Uninstalling sharex with Choco
Scoop Scoop is another Package Manager to scoop out Packages, It just Increases the variety of Packages you can Install. It stores Application in a Different Directory, In order to Sandbox them. It is a great Package Manager to Increase the Repository of Applications to Install. Anyways you can visit its Website, It has pretty easy and clear instructions to Install it. Also, you can browse Installable Applications on this page. Here is a GIF of Updating all Applications with scoop.
Conclusion Again, Installing and Using these Package Managers is really easy. So, I suggest you use these Package Managers. There are many more Package Managers like Scoop, Ninite, and Yarn. Personally, I use Chocolatey and Winget. So, I don't know about them, but you can Explore more Package Managers according to your needs.
title: Package Management

content:
Introduction Now, that we have installed Windows 10, We need to Debloat it. It is stuffed with Useless Applicatons and Packages, Used for Telemetry. Also, We will be able to make it faster as Lesser Resources will be used. Anyone can use this Guide even if you were already using your Windows Machine or If you are on Windows 11. But I recommend you to Backup your Existing Data, Just to be Safe. Ultimate Windows Utility Ultimate Windows Utility is an Free and Open Source Utility to Debloat Windows 10 and 11. It is developed by Chris Titus and Some Devs. This Utilty aims to make Debloating Windows easier as Microsoft keeps on feeding it with Bloatware.
Installing the Utility Installing the Utility is pretty easy, Just open Power Shell as an Administrator by pressing Windows Key + S and Search for Windows PowerShell, Choose to run it as an Administrator.
Next, Paste this Command to the PowerShell.
iwr -useb https://christitus.com/win | iex Usage An Intuitive Menu of the Utility will open up, Where you can Select what it does, It has Helpful Descriptions of Actions it can take, and a Very Clean GUI.
From this Menu you can Install Applications. It uses Winget to Install Applications. You can view the Logs on Other Instance of PowerShell. Then, you can shift to the tab named Tweaks.
Here, You can select the Recommended Selection according to your Hardware. Select "Desktop" if you have a Choonky Computer, Select "Laptop" if you have a Laptop, Notebook or a Windows Tablet or Select "Minimal" if you want to use it on a Tiny Machine. Else, You can just select any Option according to your needs, Just Hover over the Options to see what it does. Then Press Run Tweaks, This will Disable Telemetry Stuff and Stupid Services. You can view the Logs on Other Instance of PowerShell. Then you can Shift to the Next tab Config.
Here, You can configure your Settings and Install required Dependencies and Features. You can Install .NET Frameworks used to run Microsoft Applications, HyperV Virtualization is used to run Virtual Machines, Legacy Media (WMP, DirectPlay) is used for Background Playing, NFS is used to move files on a Network and Windows Subsystem for Linux is used to run Linux Binaries on Windows. You can Install these Features according to your need. The Fixes Section can be used to Fix existing Systems, You can Setup Autologin, Reset Windows Updates and It can Scan for Corrupt Systems. Also you can access Panels to Set-Up your System, From The Legacy Windows Panels. Next, Shift to the Update Section.
Here, You can Select settings for Updates According to your Prefrences. Select Option According to your needs, By Clicking the Option. Descriptions are given below the Options.
Conclusion This Utility is great for Debloating Windows, Kudos to Chris Titus and Devs of this Project.
description: Trimming that Fat-Ass piece of Software !
title: Debloating Windows

content:
Introduction It's an Unfortunate Event but it has happened, I have Installed Windows 10. Destroying all my FOSS Principles, I installed this Proprietary piece of Software. Just for you, Also to see how an Optimized Version of Windows works in Comparision to Linux and to fit into My Colleges Systems. Linux For an entire Year I was using Linux, What a Beautiful Experience, Hoping through Operating Systems, Optimizing it according to my needs and then Hoping on to other just to Explore its Beauty and to lose some of my Data while Trasitioning. I know, I am Pathetic. It all started with Elementary OS then Ubuntu, Kubuntu, Tiny Core, Xubuntu, Arch, Linux Mint and many more. During this Adventure I have accuired tons of Knowledge about Softwares, Hardware and Operating Systems.
Why Windows 10 ? I used Windows 10, as Windows 7 never had a chance, I know it was a Legendary Operating System. But it hasn't recieved any Security Updates after its End of Life in 2020. Also, Not a lot of People are using it, Just your Dad, That Old-Ass Computer Teacher and that Computer Repair Uncle who doesn't have an Idea on How to Update to Windows 10. Then, If you don't know Windows 8 never existed it was a myth. Windows 11, It is Crap. Its Resource Usage is too High, Hardware goes brrr....., Its stupid to Install an Operating System with this High Minimum System Requirements, Also as the Operating System is just Launched it might have a lot of Bugs and Issues. So, the Last Option was Windows 10. You weren't thinking about Windows XP? Casue if you were you need to sleep my Friend.
Now What ? Now After Installing Windows 10, I will try to Optimize it. by reducing its Resource Usage, Customizing its UI and to use a Suite of Free and Open Source Application.
Windows Installation Process After I made a Bootable USB of Windows 10, I plugged it in the USB Port and went into the Boot Menu, Selected the USB, Installation is very Easy and Basic. Kudos to Microsoft for that, I didn't connect to the Internet. So, that it won't download all the Updates and Packages for telemetry. Also, I didn't signed into my Microsoft Account. My Product Key was already present in my Hard Disk as a System Reserved Partition created by My Laptop's Manufacturer. You can add it later too, I also Disabled all the Access required by Microsoft to mine my Data to "Improve itself".
Conclusion Try not to hand over your data to those Capitalist Corporation, Your Privacy is in your Hands My Friend.
title: Installing Windows

content:
Introduction Grocery Shop Management System is a Software made to ease the management of a Grocery Shop and assists in keeping logs of Products, Customers, Inventory, and Bills. This Software can run on most CPUs, Its deployment is easy, and the UI is CLI based.
Programs used It uses Python for Menu, Data Input, and to print the Output of the Data, and MySQL is used to store the Data entered. MySQL Connector/Python is also used to Integrate Python and MySQL. Basic knowledge of Python was used while Coding the Project.
Deployement The Deployment process is very Easy, Considering you have installed Python, MySQL and MySQL Connector/Python ( If not click on each Software to See Installation Guides ). Git Clone the Repository via Terminal, with this Command.
git clone https://github.com/VighneshNilajakar/Grocery_Shop_Management_System.git After Cloning, Open INITIALIZATION.py and run it. either you can run it with a Text Editor or from The Terminal.
CAUTION : This Script clears all Data from the Database, If it already Exists.
python3 /path/to/file/Grocery_Shop_Management_System/INITIALIZATION.py Enter 'Y' or 'y if you want to run the Script, Add your MySQL Username and Passwords, and It will create all necessary Databases.
Similarly, Run MAIN.py with a Text Editor or in Terminal with this code.
python3 /path/to/file/Grocery_Shop_Management_System/MAIN.py After you Sign Into MySQL with your Credential, The CLI-based Menu is pretty clear and you can use it to maintain Product Data, Customers Data and Manufacturers Data.
Conclusion This Project isn't Fully Developed, It needs some Tweaks and Bug Fixes. It cannot be Deployed in the Real World with its Current State. No Plans for Further development are made and If you want to use this System for your Buisness, Please Consider Contacting me.
description: A Software to manage A Grocery Shop.
title: Grocery Shop Management System
